notes: Two turns separated by a /model slash-command. Turn 1 launches under model A (recipe pins --settings model=opus so A is deterministic, e.g. claude-opus-4-8); the mid-session switch uses claudecode's INLINE-ARGUMENT form `/model sonnet` (NOT the arrow-key picker — the inline alias is deterministic and recordable via a plain `slash` step), so turn 2 runs under a DIFFERENT model B (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6). The transcript carries two distinct message.model values, one per turn. MergeMetrics' 'latest non-empty/non-unknown wins' rule (session.go L627-628) promotes B after turn 2: turn 1 is attributed to A (PerTurnContribution.Model=A) and turn 2 to B (PerTurnContribution.Model=B), and SessionMetrics.ModelName flips to B by t2_end. TotalTokens accumulates across both models (T2 > T1). The /model command itself produces a <local-command> wrapper that the parser filters via isLocalCommandContent — no state machinery fires during the switch window, so the model_switch_window phase asserts that gap with duration_at_least_ms and a 'no working transition' invariant. The concrete model aliases are environment-dependent (account availability); the durable assertion is that the model CHANGES between turns and a non-empty model_name (B) is latest at session end, not a hardcoded pair.

text: Turn 1 user prompt → working under model A
Turn 1 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model A
/model slash command runs locally; <local-command> content is filtered; state stays ready, no working flicker
Turn 2 user prompt → working under model B
Turn 2 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model B (latest wins); TotalTokens after T2 > TotalTokens after T1
